questions.question: A hiking trail is $25$ miles long. If a hiker has completed $20%$ of the trail, how many miles has the hiker traveled?
A bear population in a forest increases by $15%$ from $250$ bears to $?
A car's fuel consumption decreases by $12%$ when driving on a highway. If the original fuel consumption was $25$ miles per gallon, what is the new fuel consumption?
A park ranger wants to increase the number of visitors to a national park by $18%$. If the current number of visitors is $1500$, what is the desired number of visitors?
A group of friends want to split a bill evenly, but the restaurant has a $15%$ service charge. If the total bill is $$80$, how much will each friend pay if there are $8$ friends?
A species of bird has a population of $5000$. If the population decreases by $8%$ due to habitat loss, what is the new population?
A camper's backpack weighs $25$ pounds. If the camper wants to reduce the weight by $12%$ by removing some items, how many pounds will the backpack weigh after removal?
A tourist is traveling to a country where the sales tax is $20%$. If the tourist buys an item that costs $$50$, how much will the tourist pay in total?

A company that offers guided hiking tours has $120$ hikers registered for a tour. If $25%$ of the hikers cancel due to bad weather, and the company needs to have at least $80%$ of the original number of hikers to break even, will the company break even? Show your calculations and explain your answer.

A conservation organization is trying to protect a species of animal that has a population of $2000$. If the population increases by $15%$ each year, how many animals will there be in $3$ years? Show your calculations and explain your answer.

teacher_rules: Read each question carefully before answering
Use a calculator only when necessary
Show all work for free response questions
